summary: Frontend testing of ScribFight combat overhaul (Phaser arena). Verified end-to-end flow Forge -> Lobby -> Arena. All requested HUD elements render correctly: HP bars (green), Stamina bars (cyan), Durability bar (gold), Special CD bar (purple), Round-win dots, central timer counting down from 60. Controls hint string at bottom contains dodge + special instructions. Mode label shows 'TRAINING MODE'. Two fighters spawn with seeded weapon. No console errors and no JS page errors raised during arena load and combat key presses. Reforge overlay React conditional + scene.reforgeWeapon/goBarehanded wiring inspected via code review (handleGameEvent('weaponBreak') sets reforging=true and starts 7s timer with auto bare-handed fallback) - looks correct; could not trigger naturally during ~10s test window since durability passively drains slowly (~1.5/s).

issues: Bottom controls text uses very small 9px font with #475569 color on dark bg - hard to read; consider 11-12px and lighter color

Save-to-Skills button stays disabled even after a valid drawn shape during automated mouse drag - StrokeCanvas onAnalysis appears to require continuous pointermove without sub-pixel jumps. Manual users likely fine; minor friction for testing/automation. Recommend exposing a debug hook or accepting noisier strokes.

action_items: (Optional) Increase font size/contrast of arena controls hint text
(Optional) Make StrokeCanvas analysis fire even with sparse pointer events for better automation/testing
Manual verification recommended for: ROUND 1/FIGHT intro animation, weapon break -> reforge overlay flow, KO cinematic + slow-mo, Best of 3 round transitions, win streak persistence in localStorage
critical_code_review_comments: ArenaScene.js is 800+ lines (single file) - consider splitting into modules: poses.js, attacks.js, combat.js, hud.js, fx.js for maintainability
TheArena.jsx useEffect dependency array uses eslint-disable; gameData passed via ArenaScene.pendingData (singleton on class) - if multiple arenas mount this could leak state between instances
scene.scene.restart in handleRestart re-passes data including onEvent callback - works, but pendingData remains stale on class - ok for current single-arena UX

WebGL warning 'GPU stall due to ReadPixels' - Phaser particle/texture path is calling readPixels each frame; performance impact on lower-end GPUs. Consider disabling premultipliedAlpha or pre-generating textures

weaponBreak fires onEvent only for f.id === this.f[0]?.id - correct (only local player triggers reforge UI), but in MP mode the remote opponent's weapon break is silently ignored visually beyond the shatter particles - fine for MVP
No data-testid on the Phaser-internal HUD/text (expected since Phaser draws on canvas) - reforge overlay and arena chrome correctly tagged
seed_data_creation: Seeded localStorage forge_skills with a single test weapon (TestBlade, fire, whirlwind special) and equipped index 0 to bypass forge drawing step (StrokeCanvas does not analyze automated mouse drags reliably).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The Forge StrokeCanvas analysis callback does not fire reliably with playwright synthetic pointer events, blocking the Save-to-Skills + Enter-Arena buttons. Workaround: seed localStorage 'forge_skills' (array of {name,element,vertices,allPoints,physics,special,thumbnail}) and 'forge_equipped'='0' before reload, then enter-arena-btn appears immediately. To test reforge overlay, you can call window dispatching or evaluate to call scene.weaponBreak(scene.f[0]) - but Phaser game instance is not exposed on window; easier path is to set MAX_DUR very low temporarily in code, or wait ~60s for natural durability drain. ROUND 1/FIGHT intro fades in <2.2s after StartTraining; capture screenshot at ~1.2s to see it.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. App loads cleanly, all HUD elements present, timer counts down correctly, no console/JS errors. Combat keypresses are processed by Phaser internally (cannot visually verify damage from outside canvas in short test window).
